rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees for port behaviors using visible feature names.
Implementation contracts should bind variables to subcomponent ports and connections.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the input AADL model.
Ensure AGREE expressions use types and names that match the AADL architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid component ports in their respective scopes.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use names and types consistent with the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Avoid introducing new package references or types not defined in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
